acceptedKits.name: contagion-transmission-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Model deterministic exposure, infection-stage transitions, recovery, and transmission facts for authored contagions without owning medical fiction or individual movement.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns resources, thresholds, agents, and spatial facts; this domain owns contagion-specific exposure chains and infection lifecycle meaning.

dependencies: explicit contact/exposure observations supplied by hosts or adjacent domains
plausibleUses: quarantine a spreading blight across greenhouse workers
trace a supernatural curse through witnesses in a village
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Quarantine Lantern route must replay the same contact facts into identical infection stages, transmission chains, recovery outcomes, and rejection events without browser or renderer state.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Definition registration is stable by ID; repeated exposure command IDs are ignored without adding dose or duplicate transmission facts; reset and repeated loadSnapshot converge on the same digest.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: No existing ProtoKit contains contagion, infection, epidemic, or pathogen behavior. It must not collapse into generic resource/pressure meters because transmission lineage and infection stages are the distinct reusable meaning.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until two unrelated contagion configurations, fixed-tick replay, snapshot migration, and one playable consumer pass.

acceptedKits.name: ecosystem-population-cycle-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Advance aggregate species cohorts through feeding, predation, reproduction, mortality, and carrying-capacity pressure using explicit fixed ticks and seeded resolution.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns deterministic resolution and world cells; this domain owns ecological cohort and trophic-flow meaning.

plausibleUses: restore a reef until grazer and predator populations stabilize
manage an alpine reserve through prey decline and predator recovery
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Rewilding Basin route must produce identical multi-cycle cohort totals and trophic events while two distinct authored ecosystems demonstrate reuse.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Stable IDs prevent duplicate species or feeding links; each cycle command ID commits once; reset plus the same seed and inputs reproduces the same population snapshot.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: No current kit owns carrying capacity, population cohorts, reproduction, or trophic flows. Creature rosters and agent kits own individuals or content, not aggregate ecology.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until scale proof, two ecosystem datasets, snapshot/replay proof, and clear separation from individual-agent simulation.

acceptedKits.name: structural-support-network-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve authored support graphs, loads, capacity margins, and deterministic failure propagation above provider-neutral physics observations.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core Physics owns bodies, contacts, and constraints; this domain owns gameplay-level support topology and failure meaning.

dependencies: optional normalized load/contact observations from Core Physics
plausibleUses: brace a storm-damaged suspension bridge before evacuation
undermine and reinforce fortress sections during a siege
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Bridge Under Siege route must reproduce capacity margins and the same ordered failure chain for identical support edits and load observations.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Network and observation command IDs are unique; repeated support state writes are no-ops; resolution at the same tick cannot propagate the same failure twice.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: structure-runtime-kit owns placed structure durability and operation state, while Core Physics owns constraints. Neither owns portable support graphs, capacity margins, or failure propagation.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until provider-free and Core-Physics-fed fixtures agree, large-graph budgets pass, and two structural configurations prove reuse.

acceptedKits.name: collective-resolve-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Model a group's shared resolve from member readiness, leadership, shocks, isolation, recovery, break, and rally facts without replacing individual emotions or generic meters.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns agents and pressure primitives; this domain owns group-level resolve aggregation and rally/break semantics.

dependencies: explicit semantic shock and recovery facts
plausibleUses: keep an evacuation team coordinated while infrastructure fails
break or rally a squad defending contested ground
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Bridge Under Siege route must show identical break and rally transitions from the same member, leader, and shock facts across replay.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Member state upserts replace by stable ID, shock and rally command IDs commit once, and a repeated fixed tick cannot emit duplicate threshold transitions.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: generic-pressure-loop-kit can hold a morale-shaped scalar and rpg-social-fact-kit owns individual trust/fear, but neither aggregates collective membership, leaders, shocks, break, and rally meaning.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until military and non-combat teams both prove the boundary and replay demonstrates threshold-event stability.

acceptedKits.name: territory-influence-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve authored factions' influence contributions, contest state, control transitions, and decay across portable region IDs.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns regions and deterministic state; this domain owns faction influence and control semantics.

dependencies: explicit influence contributions from adjacent domains
plausibleUses: shift a strategy frontline through supply, diplomacy, and combat facts
compete for civic districts through services and public support
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Border Accord route must replay the same contribution order into identical contested and controlled regions, including tied influence and decay cases.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Contribution command IDs apply once, region registration is stable by ID, and control transitions emit only when the resolved controller changes.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Generic defense maps own paths, slots, and vital targets; Core Spatial owns region facts. Neither owns multi-faction influence ledgers, contest rules, or control transitions.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until three-faction, neutralization, snapshot restore, and two genre configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: rumor-propagation-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track authored claims as they pass between witnesses with source lineage, credibility, distortion policy, exposure, and correction facts.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns agents, deterministic streams, and policy; this domain owns rumor lineage and belief-exposure semantics.

dependencies: core-data named deterministic stream for configured distortion
plausibleUses: spread and correct evacuation guidance through a storm town
trace compromised intelligence through rival courts
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Border Accord route must reproduce claim lineage, configured distortions, exposure sets, credibility changes, and corrections from the same share commands.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Share command IDs commit once per source-recipient pair, claim registration is stable by ID, and corrections replace state without duplicating lineage events.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: rpg-social-fact-kit owns trust, suspicion, crime, and relationships; semantic investigation owns evidence and conclusions. Neither owns transmissible claims, exposure lineage, distortion, or correction.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until deterministic distortion can be disabled and seeded, two non-dialogue consumers pass, and privacy-sensitive authored data remains outside the kit.

acceptedKits.name: navigation-knowledge-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Represent what an observer knows about places, links, hazards, closures, and route confidence separately from world truth and pathfinding.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns world, scene, and spatial truth; this domain owns observer-specific navigation knowledge and confidence.

snapshotsAndDescriptors: stale or disputed navigation fact descriptors
dependencies: explicit observations from world, scan, or authored sources
plausibleUses: chart safe mountain passages while storms invalidate old knowledge
share partial dungeon routes between separated explorers
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Lost Migration route must prove two observers can hold different known graphs, share a bounded packet, age facts, and replay identical confidence transitions.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Observation IDs update once, repeated shares merge by fact/version rather than append duplicates, and confidence decay applies once per resolved tick.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: map coverage and discovery kits track global coverage; route kits track authoritative progress. This domain is observer-specific epistemic navigation state and must not become a pathfinder.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until multi-observer merge, stale-fact, snapshot migration, and two world-structure configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: rescue-triage-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Prioritize and advance casualty stabilization, treatment, transport readiness, deterioration, and outcome facts from authored severity data.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns timers, resources, interactions, and actors; this domain owns triage priority and care-stage semantics.

dependencies: explicit semantic treatment and transport commands
plausibleUses: stabilize climbers before a storm evacuation
prioritize civilians during a collapsing-city rescue
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Quarantine Lantern route must replay the same assessment and treatment commands into identical priorities, deterioration, stabilization, and transport-ready events.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Assessment and treatment command IDs apply once, stage transitions are monotonic unless an explicit deterioration rule permits reversal, and outcomes resolve once.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: damage-health kits own health values and defeat; request queues own generic work ordering. Neither owns severity-based triage, stabilization stages, deterioration, or transport readiness.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until non-medical rescue configurations prove the generic boundary and documentation explicitly rejects real-world clinical use.

acceptedKits.name: negotiation-commitment-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Manage offers, concessions, accepted terms, obligations, deadlines, fulfillment, breach, release, and settlement as deterministic domain facts.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns ledgers, policy validation, timers, and agents; this domain owns negotiated commitment lifecycle meaning.

dependencies: core-policy guards for allowed term types
explicit fulfillment facts from adjacent domains
plausibleUses: broker safe passage between rival factions
negotiate delivery terms with dynamic obligations in a trading game
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Border Accord route must replay an offer/counter/accept sequence and identical fulfillment facts into the same obligations, breaches, releases, and settlement snapshot.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Command IDs commit once, only one terminal disposition is allowed per offer or obligation, and repeated fulfillment facts merge by obligation/version without duplicate events.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: relationship-state and social-fact kits own trust and crime facts; economy kits own value transfer. This domain owns explicit negotiated terms and obligation lifecycle without performing transfers.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until bilateral and multi-party fixtures, policy rejection, deadline replay, and two genre consumers pass.

acceptedKits.name: habitat-suitability-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Evaluate authored species or population requirements against portable regional condition observations and produce suitability, limiting-factor, and occupancy-policy facts.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns world regions and state primitives; this domain owns ecological requirement evaluation and limiting-factor meaning.

plausibleUses: select restoration sites for reef species
guide a migrating herd toward viable seasonal habitat
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Rewilding Basin route must evaluate identical region observations into identical scores and limiting factors while profile changes visibly alter occupancy eligibility.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Profiles and observations upsert by stable ID and revision, unchanged inputs reuse the same evaluation digest, and threshold events emit only on crossings.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: biome fields report world conditions and ecosystem population cycles advance cohorts. This domain only evaluates ecological fit and must not absorb either source truth or population simulation.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until two species families, invalid observation handling, snapshot migration, and composition with the population-cycle kit pass.

acceptedKits.name: wildfire-propagation-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve deterministic ignition, fuel consumption, fire-front spread, suppression, containment, and burn-state transitions across an authored portable cell graph.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns hazards, timers, random streams, world cells, and spatial facts; this domain owns wildfire-specific fuel, ignition, spread, suppression, and containment meaning.

ownership: contained, active, smoldering, and burned states
plausibleUses: contain a rail-corridor wildfire while evacuating settlements
manage magical fire spreading across linked fortress districts
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Emberline Evacuation route must reproduce identical fire fronts, fuel consumption, firebreak effects, suppression outcomes, and containment events from the same cell graph and commands.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Ignition, suppression, and firebreak command IDs commit once; registration replaces by stable region revision; a resolved tick cannot burn or spread through the same edge twice.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Hazard directors schedule generic threats and environment domains hold portable fields, but no implemented kit owns wildfire fuel, fire fronts, suppression, and containment. It must not become a generic hazard propagator or renderer.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until two unrelated fuel graphs, seeded replay, snapshot migration, scale budgets, and explicit non-predictive safety wording pass.

acceptedKits.name: power-distribution-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve a portable authored electrical service graph of sources, loads, switches, segments, faults, islands, overloads, outages, and restoration state.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns graph-capable state, resources, thresholds, and policy; this domain owns simplified gameplay electrical distribution and service-state meaning.

plausibleUses: island a storm-damaged city grid and restore critical facilities
reroute power through a derelict starship while faults cascade
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Blackout Archipelago route must replay identical switch operations, faults, load shedding, energized islands, and restored-service events without a renderer or real circuit solver.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Network revisions upsert by stable ID, switch and fault command IDs apply once, and repeated resolution at one tick cannot emit duplicate outage or restoration transitions.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: The semantic restoration DSK owns repair plans and generic utilities is only a family namespace. No implemented owner resolves sources, loads, switches, faults, electrical islands, and service allocation. The boundary stays power-specific rather than absorbing every utility.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until city and vehicle-scale networks, islanding, overload, snapshot restore, and explicit simplified-model limits pass.

acceptedKits.name: environmental-contamination-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track authored contaminants through source releases, portable region observations, concentration changes, transfer lineage, cleanup, residual risk, and clearance state.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns world regions, fields, resources, and deterministic resolution; this domain owns contaminant identity, release lineage, concentration, cleanup, residual risk, and clearance meaning.

plausibleUses: trace and clean industrial runoff across wetlands
purge magical corruption moving through connected shrine pools
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Emberline Evacuation route must replay releases, transfers, samples, cleanup, residual thresholds, and clearances into the same regional and lineage snapshot.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Release, transfer, sample, and cleanup command IDs apply once; region observations upsert by revision; clearance events emit only on resolved threshold transitions.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Contagion transmission owns subject-to-subject infection, fluid fields own provider-neutral motion data, and environment owns generic fields. This boundary owns named environmental contaminants, release lineage, cleanup, and clearance without simulating transport physics.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until water and airborne observation adapters, two fictional contaminants, snapshot migration, and safety exclusions pass.

acceptedKits.name: supply-chain-fulfillment-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Coordinate deterministic demand orders through allocation, reservation references, shipment milestones, backorders, substitutions, delivery acceptance, and fulfillment outcomes.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns ledgers, completion, routes, and policy primitives; this domain owns order-line allocation and end-to-end fulfillment lifecycle meaning across external stock and cargo owners.

dependencies: stable item, source, destination, and shipment IDs
explicit stock, reservation, cargo, and delivery facts from adjacent domains
plausibleUses: allocate scarce relief supplies across storm islands
fulfill interplanetary fabrication orders through substitute parts and delayed shipments
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Blackout Archipelago route must reproduce allocation order, backorders, substitutions, shipment milestones, accepted deliveries, and terminal fulfillment outcomes from the same facts.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Order, allocation, shipment, and milestone command IDs commit once; terminal order lines cannot reopen without a new revision; repeated acceptance cannot duplicate fulfillment.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Generic cargo owns carried items, inventory owns stock, markets own offers, and negotiated commitments own obligations. This coordinator owns fulfillment across those references and must not absorb their authoritative state.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until emergency and commercial configurations, partial fulfillment, cancellation, snapshot restore, and adjacent-owner adapters pass.

acceptedKits.name: perishability-storage-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Advance authored item batches through freshness, preservation, exposure, spoilage, and disposal-readiness transitions from explicit fixed-tick storage-condition observations.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns timers, resources, thresholds, and snapshots; this domain owns batch-level perishability, preservation, exposure accumulation, and spoilage meaning.

ownership: fresh, degraded, spoiled, and disposal-ready transitions
batch lineage through splits and merges
dependencies: stable external item and storage IDs
plausibleUses: protect medicine shipments through a rolling blackout
preserve harvested alchemical ingredients during a long expedition
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Blackout Archipelago route must replay storage changes, power-loss observations, preservation actions, batch splits, and delivery timing into identical freshness and spoilage outcomes.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Batch and condition revisions upsert by stable ID, split/merge/preserve commands apply once, and stage events emit only when the resolved batch stage changes.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: The universal catalog projects a shallow cargo-decay name, but no implemented kit owns batch lineage, storage observations, preservation, freshness stages, and spoilage. This domain must not own inventory or consumption.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until medical and non-food fictional profiles, split/merge lineage, replay, snapshot migration, and safety wording pass.

acceptedKits.name: habitat-corridor-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Evaluate ecological connectivity between suitable regions through authored corridor links, passage constraints, disruption observations, restoration, and species-profile reachability.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns world regions, spatial links, and graph facts; this domain owns ecological corridor viability and species-specific connectivity meaning.

plausibleUses: restore pollinator paths between fragmented meadows
reopen safe migration corridors for fantasy megafauna
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Pollinator Passage route must reproduce species-specific components, isolation events, restored links, and reachable habitat sets as corridor conditions change.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Graph/profile/link revisions replace by stable ID, identical evaluations reuse the same digest, and component-change events emit only when membership changes.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Navigation knowledge owns observer belief, route kits own authoritative progress, and habitat suitability scores individual regions. This domain owns ecological connectivity between regions and never computes movement paths.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until two species profiles, fragmented and cyclic graphs, snapshot migration, and composition with habitat suitability pass.

acceptedKits.name: pollination-network-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track deterministic pollinator visits, compatible pollen transfer, reproductive windows, fertilization progress, and seed-readiness facts across authored plant and pollinator profiles.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns agents, regions, interactions, timers, and deterministic resolution; this domain owns pollination visit, compatibility, fertilization, and reproductive-readiness meaning.

dependencies: stable plant, pollinator, and region IDs
plausibleUses: restore a meadow pollination web across broken habitat corridors
manage luminous moth visits needed to awaken an enchanted orchard
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Pollinator Passage route must replay visit order and reproductive windows into identical compatibility decisions, pollen lineage, fertilization progress, deficits, and readiness events.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Visit and transfer command IDs apply once, profile/window revisions replace by stable ID, and a plant can emit one readiness transition per reproductive cycle.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Ecosystem population cycles own aggregate cohorts and agriculture owns crop state; neither owns visit-level pollen transfer, compatibility, fertilization windows, or reproductive readiness.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until wild and cultivated plant profiles, multi-source pollen lineage, replay, snapshot migration, and explicit non-advisory limits pass.

acceptedKits.name: lineage-succession-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Maintain parentage and descent facts, authored inheritable entitlements, eligibility rules, precedence, succession resolution, renunciation, and transfer history.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns identities, ledgers, and policy guards; this domain owns lineage graph and succession-specific eligibility, precedence, and transfer meaning.

dependencies: authored relationship, eligibility, and entitlement facts
plausibleUses: resolve a contested royal succession after multiple renunciations
track inherited guild stewardship across adopted and biological family lines
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Crown Ledger route must replay lineage facts, eligibility changes, renunciations, and policy precedence into the same successor and transfer history.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Relationship and entitlement facts upsert by stable revision, resolution command IDs commit once, and a confirmed succession cannot resolve again without a new entitlement revision.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: RPG social facts own item ownership and interpersonal trust, while negotiation commitments own accepted obligations. Neither owns descent graphs, inherited eligibility, precedence, renunciation, or succession outcomes.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until biological and non-biological lineage structures, disputed eligibility, cycle rejection, snapshot migration, and two genre configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: governance-resolution-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve authored proposals through eligibility, quorum, ballots, voting rules, abstention, tie policy, ratification, enactment status, and decision history.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns identities, policy validation, timers, and ledgers; this domain owns collective proposal, quorum, ballot, tally, ratification, and enactment lifecycle meaning.

dependencies: stable body, participant, and proposal IDs
explicit enactment facts from adjacent domains
plausibleUses: settle a council succession crisis through weighted houses
let a cooperative colony vote on emergency resource policy
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Crown Ledger route must replay eligibility, ballots, abstentions, quorum shifts, ties, ratification, and enactment facts into an identical decision history.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Ballot command IDs commit once and replace only under explicit revision policy, close and ratify commands are terminal once, and enactment facts merge by decision revision.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Core Policy validates allowed actions and negotiation owns party commitments; no implemented kit owns proposal voting, quorum, tally, ratification, and enactment state. Authored political rules remain data.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until simple-majority and weighted-rule bodies, tie/quorum edge cases, snapshot migration, replay, and non-real-world framing pass.

acceptedKits.name: language-comprehension-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Evaluate authored languages and dialects, mutual-intelligibility relationships, participant proficiency, semantic message facets, comprehension confidence, misunderstanding, and learning evidence.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns agents, data, policy, and optional inference requests; this domain owns authored language proficiency and deterministic semantic-comprehension meaning without generating or translating text.

dependencies: optional external inference observations supplied as facts
plausibleUses: decode a damaged alien warning through repeated contextual evidence
negotiate safe passage across mutually intelligible fantasy dialects
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Broken Tongues route must replay proficiency, dialect relationships, message facets, clarifications, and learning evidence into identical comprehension and mastery outcomes without generating text.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Language, intelligibility, and proficiency facts upsert by stable revision; assessment and clarification command IDs apply once; mastery events emit only on threshold crossings.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Dialogue kits own authored line flow, rumor propagation owns claim exposure, and MLNN owns inference requests. No implemented kit owns language/dialect proficiency, mutual intelligibility, semantic facet comprehension, and learning evidence.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until fictional dialect and code-language configurations, clarification, snapshot migration, deterministic replay, and inference-free operation pass.

acceptedKits.name: flood-inundation-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve deterministic water-volume arrival, cell inundation depth, barrier overtopping, drainage, recession, and dry-state transitions across an authored portable cell graph.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns cells, volumes, observations, and deterministic resolution; this domain owns flood-specific water balance, inundation, overtopping, drainage, and recession meaning.

ownership: inundation depth and band per cell
dependencies: explicit inflow, barrier, and drainage facts
plausibleUses: manage levees and evacuation corridors through a rising river delta
drain a breached underground habitat without simulating rendered fluid
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Delta Under Siege route must replay identical inflows, barrier edits, and drainage facts into the same inundation depths, overtopping order, recession history, and rejected commands.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Flow and barrier command IDs apply once, graph registration is stable by ID, and each fixed tick emits a depth-band or dry transition only when resolved state changes.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: water-data-kit declares water bodies and water-physics-kit owns buoyancy/surface sampling, while terrain erosion owns material response. None owns portable flood-volume propagation, overtopping, inundation lifecycle, and recession.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until mass balance, branching overflow, restore/replay, large-cell budgets, and two non-rendered flood configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: soil-nutrient-cycle-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track authored nutrient pools, transformations, transfers, uptake, return, depletion, and recovery across portable land units without owning crop growth or farm operations.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns resources and deterministic ticks; this domain owns nutrient-pool mass balance and cycling semantics above portable land observations.

ownership: available, bound, and organic nutrient quantities
dependencies: explicit uptake, return, and transfer facts
plausibleUses: restore exhausted terraces through compost and crop rotation inputs
rebalance alien soil before a colony seed bank can germinate
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Seed Ark route must replay nutrient inputs, uptake, return, and transformations into identical pool balances, limiting factors, depletion, and recovery events across farm and wild-land configurations.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Facts merge by stable command ID and revision, each cycle consumes a fact once, and reset plus the same authored profile and input order reproduces the same mass-balance digest.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: agriculture-domain-kit owns plot fertility, watering, crop growth, and harvest; this boundary owns explicit nutrient pools and transfers across farm or wild land and must expose observations agriculture may consume rather than replace its soil API.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until nutrient mass balance, agricultural and restoration consumers, snapshot migration, deterministic replay, and bounded-history scale proof pass.

acceptedKits.name: genetic-diversity-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Maintain authored population trait-marker frequencies, parent contribution records, diversity measures, inbreeding risk, and deterministic cohort inheritance outcomes.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns identities, ledgers, and deterministic random streams; this domain owns population-genetic diversity, contribution, and inheritance meaning.

plausibleUses: preserve diversity while rebuilding an endangered herd
manage fictional bloodline traits in a creature-breeding strategy game without owning succession rights
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Seed Ark route must resolve the same source contributions into identical marker frequencies, diversity scores, risk transitions, and cohort summaries for wildlife and fantasy-creature datasets.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Contribution and cohort command IDs commit once, population merges consume explicit source revisions, and the same seed and ordered facts reproduce identical frequencies and descriptors.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: lineage-succession-domain-kit owns descent and inherited eligibility, while ecosystem population owns cohort counts and trophic cycles. Neither owns marker frequencies, relatedness, diversity measures, or cohort inheritance summaries.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until fictional-only policy, two unrelated datasets, deterministic cohort replay, snapshot migration, and numerical-stability budgets pass.

acceptedKits.name: seed-dispersal-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track authored biological seed release, carrier pickup, transport lineage, deposition, viability, dormancy, and germination-readiness facts across portable regions.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns deterministic streams, agents, regions, and observations; this domain owns biological seed dispersal lineage and readiness meaning.

dependencies: stable biological seed, carrier, and region IDs
optional core-data deterministic stream for authored viability variation
plausibleUses: guide animal-carried seeds through a fragmented rewilding corridor
recover a wind-scattered alien seed vault before dormancy expires
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Seed Ark route must replay release, pickup, deposition, and exposure facts into identical lineage, viability, dormancy, and readiness outcomes for animal-carried and wind-observation configurations.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Batch, pickup, and deposition command IDs commit once, exposure facts upsert by revision, and readiness emits only on a false-to-true transition.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: generic-seed-kit owns random-stream state, pollination owns pollen transfer and fertilization, and agriculture owns planted crops and growth. None owns biological seed transport lineage, viability, dormancy, and deposition readiness.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until two carrier modes, lineage replay, snapshot restore, bounded batch history, and downstream habitat or agriculture consumption pass.

acceptedKits.name: aquifer-balance-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve authored groundwater stores through recharge, extraction, transfer, drawdown, recovery, well availability, and intrusion-risk state without simulating subsurface fluid physics.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns resources, cells, and deterministic resolution; this domain owns groundwater storage, recharge, extraction, and availability semantics.

dependencies: portable aquifer, well, and region IDs
plausibleUses: ration a desert settlement's wells while seasonal recharge arrives
balance lunar ice-lens extraction against habitat reserves
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Deepwell Accord route must replay recharge, withdrawals, transfers, and governance-approved limits into identical drawdown, well availability, recovery, and risk transitions.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Recharge, extraction, and transfer command IDs commit once, availability bands transition once per resolved change, and reset plus the same ordered facts reproduces exact storage balances.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: water-data-kit declares surface water bodies and generic resource loops own scalar meters. Neither owns aquifer/well topology, recharge and withdrawal lineage, drawdown recovery, or well availability.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until balance conservation, multiple connected aquifers, restore/replay, scale proof, and two climate-independent configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: potable-water-service-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Coordinate authored raw-water intake, treatment milestones, safe storage, demand allocation, service loss, leak loss, and potable-delivery acceptance across a portable service graph.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns resources, policy checks, and scene identities; this domain owns potable-water treatment, safety clearance, allocation, and service lifecycle meaning.

ownership: authored intake, treatment, storage, and demand nodes
leak loss and delivery acceptance ledger
snapshotsAndDescriptors: demand allocation, leak, outage, and delivery descriptors
dependencies: explicit safety-clearance facts from contamination or host policy
plausibleUses: restore safe water service across blackout islands
operate a sealed colony's treatment loop after a contaminated intake
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Deepwell Accord route must replay intake, treatment, clearance, allocation, leak, and delivery commands into identical safe storage, outages, accepted deliveries, and rejections.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Treatment and delivery commands advance each batch milestone once, allocation command IDs commit once, and repeated clearance facts at the same revision emit no duplicate service transition.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: power-distribution owns electrical service and supply-chain fulfillment owns orders and shipments. Water data/physics kits own water descriptions and physical interaction. None owns potable treatment, clearance, storage, allocation, outage, and delivery acceptance.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until contamination-fed and provider-free fixtures, allocation fairness, snapshot migration, replay, and two service-graph configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: thermal-energy-network-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve authored thermal zones through heat capacity, source and sink energy, conductive links, losses, equilibrium bands, and deterministic overheat or freeze transitions.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns scalar resources, observations, and deterministic ticks; this domain owns thermal energy balance and transfer-network meaning.

dependencies: optional normalized ambient and contact observations
plausibleUses: balance reactor heat through a failing orbital station
keep a frostline greenhouse above freezing during rolling power loss
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Frostline Habitat route must replay source, link, ambient, and power-service facts into identical energy balances, threshold transitions, equilibrium events, and rejected commands.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Source, sink, link, and observation command IDs apply once per revision, fixed ticks resolve in stable zone/link order, and threshold events emit only on band transitions.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: generic-pressure-loop-kit can represent a heat scalar and Core Physics owns provider contracts, but neither owns multi-zone heat capacity, energy conservation, transfer links, equilibrium, or thermal recovery meaning.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until conservation tolerance, cyclic networks, snapshot/replay, large-zone budgets, and habitat plus industrial configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: habitat-atmosphere-balance-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track authored sealed compartments through gas inventories, occupant demand, leaks, exchange links, scrubber and generator effects, and breathable-state transitions.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns resources, scene objects, agents, and deterministic resolution; this domain owns sealed-habitat gas balance and breathability meaning.

exclusions: sky, fog, cloud, wind, or precipitation descriptors
dependencies: stable compartment, gas, and occupant-group IDs
plausibleUses: seal and rebalance compartments after a station hull breach
manage a deep-mine refuge's air scrubbers during an evacuation
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Frostline Habitat route must replay occupants, leaks, exchange links, scrubber effects, and power observations into identical gas fractions, reserve estimates, safety transitions, and recovery.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Leak, exchange, occupant, and processor facts upsert by stable revision, fixed ticks resolve compartments in stable order, and breathability events emit only when classification changes.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: atmosphere-domain-service-kit owns fog, sky, clouds, wind, precipitation, and visibility descriptors; generic pressure owns scalar oxygen debt. Neither owns sealed-compartment gas inventories, exchange, processing, and breathability classification.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until mass balance, multi-compartment exchange, snapshot migration, replay, and station plus terrestrial-refuge configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: seismic-event-sequence-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Advance authored fault segments through stress observations, rupture eligibility, deterministic event sequencing, intensity fields, aftershock scheduling, and quiet-state recovery.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns timers, spatial facts, and physics observations; this domain owns seismic stress, rupture, intensity, aftershock, and recovery semantics.

dependencies: core-data named deterministic stream for configured aftershock variation
optional normalized physics or terrain observations
plausibleUses: stabilize a mountain relay through a main shock and aftershock window
manage a subterranean city as connected fault segments rupture
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Faultline Relay route must replay stress and rupture commands into identical fault order, regional intensity bands, aftershock schedule, quiet-state recovery, and rejections without physics or renderer ownership.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Stress and rupture command IDs commit once, aftershocks derive from a named stream and stable event ID, and each event or quiet transition emits once at its resolved tick.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Core Physics owns normalized contacts and camera-shake-kit owns presentation feedback, while structural support owns failure outcomes. No existing kit owns a fault stress ledger, rupture sequence, regional intensity, and aftershock lifecycle.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until deterministic mainshock/aftershock replay, snapshot restore, multi-fault scale proof, and two authored geology configurations pass.

acceptedKits.name: orbital-transfer-window-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Evaluate authored celestial-body and vehicle-state observations into deterministic maneuver opportunities, transfer cost and duration estimates, intercept tolerance, reservation, and arrival-window outcomes.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns trajectories, physics providers, spatial facts, and timers; this domain owns gameplay-level orbital transfer opportunity, reservation, and arrival-window meaning without moving bodies.

ownership: arrival-window success, miss, and expiry history
dependencies: stable body, vehicle, profile, and observation IDs
explicit ephemeris observations from a host or physics provider
plausibleUses: schedule a rescue tug between failing orbital habitats
route a fantasy skyship between moving moon-islands using authored orbital observations
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Skyhook Rescue route must replay ephemeris revisions, opportunity evaluation, reservations, burn facts, and arrival resolution into identical windows, estimates, misses, expiries, and accepted arrivals.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Observation revisions replace by stable source version, evaluation and reservation command IDs commit once, and arrival outcomes become terminal until an explicit reset or new reservation.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Core Motion owns trajectories and Core Physics owns provider frames; route and cargo kits own checkpoints and transfers. None owns orbital opportunity evaluation, transfer-window reservation, intercept tolerance, and arrival outcome semantics.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until provider-supplied and analytic-fixture observations agree, replay and snapshot migration pass, and two fictional orbital configurations prove reuse.

rejectedCandidates.name: signal-triangulation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing universal game-domain inventory already declares triangulation-kit inside survey/cartography map coverage, and acoustic-signal plus scan/survey families already own adjacent signal observations.

rejectedCandidates.name: investigation-evidence-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already installs engine.n.investigation and owns cases, observations, evidence, hypotheses, and conclusions.

rejectedCandidates.name: crafting-transformation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: survival-crafting-domain already declares crafting-domain-service-kit plus recipe tables, inputs, outputs, stations, and failure rules.

rejectedCandidates.name: shelter-exposure-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: survival-needs and weather domain services already own temperature and exposure descriptors, while generic resource/pressure kits own the reusable scalar transitions.

rejectedCandidates.name: crowd-flow-demand-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Current NexusEngine already exports promoted OccupantFlowKit and RequestQueueKit operations semantics; a new ProtoKit would be a rename without a distinct higher-level responsibility.

rejectedCandidates.name: formation-assignment-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing Agent Group domain explicitly owns groups, agents, goals, basic movement, and formation state; a slot-assignment-only candidate did not clear the adjacent-domain split gate.

rejectedCandidates.name: infrastructure-restoration-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already installs engine.n.restoration and owns assets, diagnosis, plans, work contributions, commissioning, snapshots, and reset.

rejectedCandidates.name: stealth-suspicion-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: sensory-agent-domain-kit and perception-kit already own reusable detection observations, while rpg-social-fact-kit owns suspicion; the candidate added no coherent semantic owner above those boundaries.

rejectedCandidates.name: item-ownership-transfer-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: rpg-social-fact-kit already exposes ownership set, theft, return, crime, and suspicion facts for items; a transfer-only wrapper would duplicate that implemented surface.

rejectedCandidates.name: utility-network-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A generic utility graph blurred electricity, water, fuel, data, repair, and service semantics. It was split down to the accepted power-distribution boundary with explicit exclusions.

rejectedCandidates.name: incident-priority-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core RequestQueue/priority operations plus the accepted rescue-triage severity boundary already cover generic ordering and casualty priority; the candidate supplied no additional incident lifecycle meaning.

rejectedCandidates.name: signal-relay-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: acoustic-signal-domain-kit is implemented and universal-game-domain-kits already declares signal strength, sync, relay, scramble, and beacon-link surfaces; a new relay wrapper would deepen an existing overlap cluster.

rejectedCandidates.name: soil-fertility-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: agriculture-domain-kit already owns farm plot soil preparation, moisture, fertility, watering, growth, and harvest. The accepted replacement is limited to explicit nutrient-pool mass balance across farm or wild land and excludes agriculture operations.

rejectedCandidates.name: atmosphere-weather-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: atmosphere-domain-service-kit already owns renderer-agnostic fog, sky, clouds, wind, precipitation, and visibility descriptors. The accepted habitat-atmosphere boundary instead owns sealed-compartment gas inventories and breathability.

rejectedCandidates.name: flood-water-body-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: water-data-kit already declares water bodies and profiles for ponds, rivers, oceans, floods, and pools. The accepted inundation boundary owns water-volume propagation, barrier overtopping, drainage, and recession rather than another water-body record.

rejectedCandidates.name: heat-pressure-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core Simulation and generic-pressure-loop-kit already own heat-shaped scalar resources, rates, thresholds, and transitions. The accepted thermal-energy network adds multi-zone capacity, transfer links, conservation, and equilibrium semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: orbital-trajectory-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core Motion owns trajectories and root-motion frames while Core Physics owns provider frames. The accepted orbital-transfer-window boundary consumes observations and owns only maneuver opportunity, reservation, and arrival-window semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: water-utility-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A generic water utility blurred aquifer storage, potable treatment, distribution, fluid physics, contamination, and repair. It was split into aquifer-balance and potable-water-service boundaries with explicit cross-domain facts.
